paper_title: GPNAS: A Neural Network Architecture Search Framework Based on Graphical Predictor

method_section.problem_statement: To find an optimal network architecture that satisfies certain constraints, primarily by defining a search space.

related_work_critique.evolutionary_algorithms: Require a large amount of computation.
related_work_critique.reinforcement_learning_rl: Reduced calculation cost but failed to solve the problem of credit allocation.
related_work_critique.bohb: Requires strong manual prior and model design ability, defeating the original purpose of NAS.
related_work_critique.differentiable_nas_liou: The searcher's training and evaluation goals are not unified, which may lead to overfitting.
related_work_critique.alternate_rl_jiang: Analysis of the skip-connection search path has large noise and is inefficient.
related_work_critique.predictor_liou_25: The LSTM-based predictor can only predict performance of child models without skip-connections, limiting its versatility.

method_section.general_observation: Previous methods are only upgraded in a certain aspect and make unnecessary compromises when applied to practical work.

results_section.summary: The authors state that previous works are limited and aim to create a more comprehensive solution.

contributions.title: Alternate Neural Network Search Framework
contributions.description: Decouples the search space of skip-connection and operator.
contributions.methodology: Adopts BOHB as the searcher instead of RL to improve efficiency.

contributions.title: GCN-based Predictor
contributions.description: A predictor designed using Graph Convolutional Networks (GCN).
contributions.status: The provided text is truncated and does not detail its full function.

title: GPNAS: A Neural Network Architecture Search Framework Based on Graphical Predictor
